title: Song Of The Night At Daybreak
content:
All my stars forsake me,
    And the dawn-winds shake me.
    Where shall I betake me?

    Whither shall I run
    Till the set of sun,
    Till the day be done?

    To the mountain-mine,
    To the boughs o' the pine,
    To the blind man's eyne,

    To a brow that is
    Bowed upon the knees,
    Sick with memories.
meaning:
Alice Christiana Thompson Meynell's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Song Of The Night At Daybreak"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
